background_template: {name} has run a deli counter out of {location} since {founded}. One long counter, a cold case, and a lunch rush that starts at noon sharp. {quirk}
neighbourhoods: a narrow unit on the market row
the corner of the high street
a railway arch by the station
a shopfront next to the town hall
the old cheesemonger's on Cross Street
quirks: The counter still slices everything to order, and the lunch queue has made its peace with that.
The owner drives to Italy every August and comes back with a car full of things nobody asked for.
They have kept the same handwritten specials board since the day they opened.
Half the regulars order the exact same sandwich daily and take it personally when it is off.
The owner knows everyone's usual and will gently judge you for changing it.
Everything gets wrapped in paper and string, which takes ages, and the regulars would riot if it stopped.
pains: we lose lunch orders because the phone does not stop between twelve and two
people ask what is in every sandwich and we answer the same thing forty times a day
we cannot keep the prices on the counter and the prices online in step
half our regulars want to know what is vegetarian before they have reached the counter
people turn up asking for things we only put out on certain days

products.name: Buffalo Mozzarella
products.description: In brine. Use it today, not tomorrow.

products.name: Wild Boar Salami
products.description: Coarse cut, gamey, a little fennel.

products.name: Marinated Gordal Olives
products.description: Fat, green, more garlic than sense.

products.name: Truffle & Porcini Pâté
products.description: More truffle than is strictly reasonable.

products.name: Sun-dried Tomato Focaccia
products.description: Baked next door, in by nine.

products.name: Roasted Veg & Hummus Wrap
products.description: The vegetarian one people actually reorder.

products.name: Balsamic, 12-Year
products.description: Thick, syrupy, worth the shelf space.

awkward.name: Aged Parmigiano Reggiano, Vacche Rosse, 30-Month (Whole Wedge, Cut to Order)
awkward.description: The good one. We get a handful of wheels a year.

awkward.name: Jamón Ibérico & Manchego Selection
awkward.description: Sells out most Saturdays before noon.

features.name: product listing
features.description: Show the whole counter — everything we sell, the price, and what is in it. That is the entire point of the page.

features.name: contact form
features.description: Let people put in a lunch order or ask a question without ringing the counter.

features.name: opening hours
features.description: Put our hours where people will actually see them. We are shut Sundays.

features.name: category filter
features.description: People want just the cheese, or just the sandwiches. Let them narrow it down.

features.name: allergen information
features.description: We get asked what is in everything all day. Put it on the page so we can stop repeating ourselves.

features.name: navigation
features.description: A way to move around the page. Nothing fancy.

constraints: It has to work on a phone. Most of our lunch orders come from people walking over on their break.
Our brand colour is #3A5A40. Please use it.
No carousels. We had one once; nobody ever saw the second slide.
Don't invent products. If it isn't on the counter, it doesn't go on the page.
The prices on the page have to match the file we sent. To the penny.
Nothing that plays sound on its own. We're a deli.
